seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: New features include Developer Mode for browser with CDP access, deeper debugging tools, /init in app composer, customizable icons, enterprise Computer Use, Windows controls, faster browser use, and rate-limit reset banking for Plus/Pro users.
Google's upcoming 3.5 model is expected to be the cheapest SOTA, used internally with high satisfaction; positioned as a strong competitor in the model landscape.

Focus on stacking loops (goals and orchestration) that scale with more agents rather than fixing issues manually; emphasizes going 'up' loops for leverage as models improve, per the 'Salty Lesson' for agents.
Need for platforms that close the loop on errors and failures automatically, reducing 'webmaster' infra setup for every project by integrating monitoring, alerts, and orchestration into one system.

global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Shift to structured, persistent memory layers instead of overloading context windows for more scalable production AI agents and long-term recall.
Composable pipelines (FFmpeg-style) for workflow automation and multi-model orchestration; event-sourced memory layer to improve reliability, state management, and reproducibility in agent systems.

global_discoveries.project: Recursive Agent Harnesses and related papers
global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: LLM turned into stack of cooperating agents that spawn sub-agents, use tools, and reason over long contexts; plus operadic consistency for self-detecting reasoning errors and analogy-based retrieval for learning reusable skills.

near_misses.project: Decentralized AI stack (Akash, Bittensor, Fetch.ai etc.)
near_misses.technical_takeaway: Open compute markets, decentralized models, data ownership, and permissionless agents as alternative to centralized AI.
near_misses.reason: Interesting high-level overview but lacks specific technical details or new repo/framework release.

notes: Seed experts yielded several relevant updates on tools and agent patterns; global search surfaced memory/orchestration innovations. Limited truly novel open-source releases in the window; focused on actionable technical insights.
